perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means technical and non- technical information, data, software, records, customer information, financial information, practices, processes, methods, techni ques, trade secrets, products, and/or research and any other item that (a) is of a confidential nature, (b) would reasonably be considered as confidential, (c) is clearly and conspicuously marked as confidential or (d) is identified orally by the disclosing Party as confidential, and which in each case is disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party in relation to the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means technical and non- technical information, data, software, records, customer information, financial information, practices, processes, methods, techni ques, trade secrets, products, and/or research and any other item that (a) is of a confidential nature, (b) would reasonably be considered as confidential, (c) is clearly and conspicuously marked as confidential or (d) is identified orally by the disclosing Party as confidential, and which in each case is disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party in relation to the Intended Interaction.
perturbation.explanation: The term “Purpose” is changed to “Intended Interaction” in the definition of Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because other sections still refer to 'Purpose,' making it unclear which definition applies and whether the scope of permitted use is limited to the Proposed Transaction or something broader. “Purpose” is defined as the use of Confidential Information for the purpose of the Parties considering whether they should enter into the Proposed Transaction, while the rest of the document keeps mentioning 'Purpose', thus creating an ambiguity on what definition to follow.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees not to use the Confidential I nformation in any fashion, form, or manner for any purpose other than the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Purpose" to "Intended Interaction" in the definition of "Confidential Information" directly contradicts the clause "Each Party agrees not to use the Confidential I nformation in any fashion, form, or manner for any purpose other than the Purpose." The original text consistently uses "Purpose," which is specifically defined. The changed text creates a misalignment of terminology by introducing "Intended Interaction" within the definition of "Confidential Information" while the contradicted text uses "Purpose", creating ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction. The definition of 'Purpose' is the use of Confidential Information for considering whether the parties should enter into the proposed transaction. By changing 'Purpose' to 'Intended Interaction' in the definition of Confidential Information, the document now has conflicting terms and it is uncertain which term to follow.

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Materials, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" is changed to "Confidential Materials" only at the beginning of the sentence in section 6. The remaining mentions of it keep the term 'Confidential Information'. This creates confusion as to whether the return obligation applies to all confidential information or only a subset now defined as 'Confidential Materials.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Materials, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' at the beginning of the sentence in the Changed Text, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the latter part of the same sentence, creates a contradiction. The Contradicted Text uses the new term 'Confidential Materials'. This misalignment in terminology creates uncertainty regarding which type of information the Receiving Party is obligated to return or destroy, as it's unclear if the obligation now only applies to 'Confidential Materials' or the broader 'Confidential Information', fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreement for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Proprietary Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" is changed to "Proprietary Information" in the survival clause. While 'Confidential Information' is defined, 'Proprietary Information' is not. This creates uncertainty about what information remains protected after termination, because it is uncertain which information is protected from each category, generating a contradiction to what to follow.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreement for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Proprietary Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is defined. The changed text replaces this with 'Proprietary Information,' which is not defined. This creates a contradiction because the survival clause in the agreement now refers to an undefined term. The Contradicted Text uses 'Proprietary Information', furthering the undefined term, and therefore the agreement does not clearly specify which information survives termination, making it difficult to enforce and causing a contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that irreparable harm may occur if any of the Confidential Information wer e to be disclosed to third parties or if any use were to be made of the Confidential Information other than that specified in this Agreement, and the Parties further agree that each shall have the right to seek and obtain injunctive relief upon any violati on or threatened violation of the terms of this Agreement, in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that substantial damages may occur if any of the Confidential Information wer e to be disclosed to third parties or if any use were to be made of the Confidential Information other than that specified in this Agreement, and the Parties further agree that each shall have the right to seek and obtain injunctive relief upon any violati on or threatened violation of the terms of this Agreement, in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The term “irreparable harm” is replaced by “substantial damages.” While the paragraph still states the Parties can seek injunctive relief, the change from 'irreparable harm' to 'substantial damages' undermines the basis for injunctive relief, as it suggests monetary damages could be sufficient compensation. It creates an in-text contradiction on the appropriate remedy available in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that substantial damages may occur if any of the Confidential Information wer e to be disclosed to third parties or if any use were to be made of the Confidential Information other than that specified in this Agreement, and the Parties further agree that each shall have the right to seek and obtain injunctive relief upon any violati on or threatened violation of the terms of this Agreement, in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'irreparable harm,' which supports injunctive relief. The changed text replaces this with 'substantial damages,' implying that monetary compensation might suffice. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, so the change creates a contradiction by undermining the basis for injunctive relief within the contract, as it no longer clearly establishes that monetary damages are insufficient.
